name: ClawMax Dev Team
description: Self-managing development team for ClawMax. Agents triage issues, review PRs, run tests, and prepare releases. Pre-configured for the ClawMax repo with sensible defaults.
author: ClawMax Team

agents.name: Dev Lead
agents.role: Development lead — coordinates the team, reviews architecture decisions, approves PRs, manages releases. You are the technical decision-maker for the ClawMax project.

agents.name: QA Engineer
agents.role: Quality assurance — runs test suites, reports failures, verifies fixes, maintains test coverage. You ensure every change passes tests before merge.

agents.name: GitHub Triage
agents.role: Issue and PR triage — labels new issues, assigns priority, routes to the right team member, ensures nothing falls through the cracks. You are the first responder for all GitHub activity.

agents.name: Release Engineer
agents.role: Release management — prepares changelogs, tags releases, verifies builds, updates version numbers. You ensure smooth, documented releases.

groups.name: Testing
groups.description: Test results, coverage reports, QA updates

groups.name: Triage
groups.description: Issue triage, bug reports, feature requests

workflows.name: Dev Team Kickoff
workflows.description: Initialize the dev team and set up GitHub coordination
workflows.content:
# Dev Team Kickoff

You are the Dev Lead. Your team just came online.

## Project Configuration
> **Customize before applying:**

- **GitHub repo:** [owner/repo-name]
- **Main branch:** [main]
- **Test command:** [./SYSTEM/test.sh]
- **Release prefix:** [v]
- **Sprint duration:** [1 week]

## Your Tasks
1. Introduce yourself in the Dev Team community
2. Review the current state of the GitHub repo (open issues, PRs, recent commits)
3. Assign initial responsibilities:
   - GitHub Triage: scan and label all open issues
   - QA Engineer: run the test suite and report results
   - Release Engineer: check the latest tag and pending changes
4. Set the sprint goal based on open high-priority issues
5. Post the sprint plan to Dev Status group

## GitHub Coordination
Use the configured repo for all work.

- Create GitHub issues for tasks and assignments
- Push code changes to branches
- Open PRs for review
- Track progress via issue comments

workflows.name: Issue Triage
workflows.description: Scan and triage new GitHub issues daily
workflows.content:
# Daily Issue Triage

1. Check the GitHub repo for new issues since last triage
2. For each new issue:
   - Add appropriate labels (bug, feature, enhancement, documentation)
   - Set priority (high-priority, medium-priority, low-priority)
   - Assign to the right team member based on content
3. Check for stale issues (no activity in 7+ days) and ping assignees
4. Post triage summary to Triage group

## GitHub Coordination
Use the configured repo for all work.

workflows.name: Test Run
workflows.description: Run test suite and report results
workflows.content:
# Daily Test Run

1. Pull the latest code from the main branch
2. Run the full test suite using the configured test command
3. Report results:
   - Total tests, passed, failed, warnings
   - Any new failures since last run
   - Test duration
4. If failures found:
   - Create or update GitHub issues for each failure
   - Tag with 'bug' and 'test-failure' labels
   - Assign to the dev-lead for triage
5. Post test summary to Testing group

## GitHub Coordination
Use the configured repo for all work.

workflows.name: PR Review
workflows.content:
# PR Review Session

## Dev Lead Tasks
1. List all open PRs in the GitHub repo
2. For each PR:
   - Review the changes and description
   - Check if tests pass
   - Leave review comments or approve
3. Merge approved PRs

## QA Engineer Tasks
1. For each open PR:
   - Check if CI passes
   - Review test coverage
   - Flag any missing tests
2. Post review notes to Code Review group

## GitHub Coordination
Use the configured repo for all work.

workflows.name: Release Prep
workflows.description: Prepare and tag a new release
workflows.content:
# Release Preparation

## Release Engineer Tasks
1. Check the latest tag and list all commits since then
2. Generate a changelog from commit messages
3. Verify all tests pass on the latest commit
4. Bump version number if needed
5. Create the release tag
6. Post release notes to Release group

## Dev Lead Tasks
1. Review the changelog for completeness
2. Approve the release
3. Announce in Dev Status group

## GitHub Coordination
Use the configured repo for all work.
